source.title: SafeDreamer: Safe Reinforcement Learning with World Models

confidence_rationale: SafeDreamer implements MECH-056 structurally in an ML system: cost/safety signals are added as a separate prediction head (cost decoder) operating on shared RSSM world-model representations, and safety constraints are applied at the trajectory-planning stage (OSRP: Online Safety-Reward Planning) rather than by modifying the representation learning objective. This is a direct computational demonstration that trajectory-level harm constraint and core state representation learning can be functionally decoupled. The OSRP planner filters trajectories based on cost thresholds before returning an action, realising the trajectory-first principle. Confidence is moderate because the paper does not explicitly motivate this separation as a representational integrity principle -- it is an engineering choice -- and the world model still has a cost prediction loss that backpropagates through shared representations, meaning the separation is partial rather than absolute.

mapping.source_claim_statement: SafeDreamer incorporates Lagrangian-based methods into DreamerV3 world model planning. Cost and reward are separate prediction heads on shared RSSM states. The OSRP planner filters candidate trajectories: only trajectories with expected cost below threshold enter the safe action set, from which reward-maximising actions are selected.
mapping.ree_translation: Supports MECH-056 computationally: safety/harm signals (REE residue) are represented as separate decoder outputs on the shared world-model state, not as modifications to the state encoder itself. Trajectory selection (OSRP planning) applies the safety filter at selection time -- the harm criterion gates which trajectory is chosen, not which state representation is learned. This is a working engineering implementation of the trajectory-first principle, albeit without an explicit commitment to representational integrity as a design goal.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The cost loss (−β_c ln C_phi(c_t|s_t)) does backpropagate through shared RSSM representations, so separation is architectural (separate heads) rather than gradient-level (stop-gradient). The paper does not test whether representations are corrupted by the cost signal relative to a baseline without it. The MECH-056 claim requires that harm signals not distort core representations; SafeDreamer achieves partial separation by design but does not claim or verify full representational preservation.
mapping.source_context: ICLR 2024 paper. Built on DreamerV3 (Hafner 2023). Safety-Gymnasium benchmarks. Achieves near-zero-cost performance. OSRP planner maintains a safe action set at each planning step.

confidence_components.notes: Strong ICLR venue, solid engineering results. Mapping fidelity is good for the trajectory-selection aspect but partial for the representational preservation aspect -- cost gradients flow through shared representations. Transfer risk moderate: this is an engineering system, not a biological system, but it directly implements the algorithmic pattern MECH-056 describes.
